question.text: A 'tag along' right in an AIF investment agreement allows:

answer.text: The fund manager to force all investors to sell
Minority investors to join a sale if majority shareholders sell their stake
The investee company to buy back shares at any time
The fund to increase its stake automatically

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tag along (co-sale) rights allow minority investors to participate in a sale initiated by majority shareholders on the same terms and conditions. This prevents majority shareholders from selling out while leaving minority investors trapped. **The Trap:** Tag along is different from drag along. Tag along protects minorities (right to join a sale); drag along empowers majorities (right to force minorities to sell). **Cross-Exam Context:** Tag along and drag along rights are fundamental investor protection mechanisms tested across XIX-series exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Tag along rights are critical for AIF investors who are typically minority shareholders. Without tag along, a promoter could sell their controlling stake to a buyer who might not value minority interests.
